title: Localization of the Human CXC Chemokine Subfamily on the Long Arm of Chromosome 4 Using Radiation Hybrids,
author: Modi, William S.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-zero
description: All 12 of the human CXC chemokine genes were physically mapped using gene-specific PCR primers and the GenBridge 4 radiation hybrid panel. Nine genes, PF4, PF4V1, GRO1, GCP2, PPBP, IL8, GRO2, GRO3, and SCYB5, were assigned within a 1.8-cR interval of one another on 4q. Two additional genes, MIG and INP10, map within 0.5 cR of each another and 6 cR distal to the above-mentioned group. The final gene, SDF1, is localized on 10q. Phylogenetic analyses of amino acid sequences revealed that SDF1 is the most divergent member and that the physically separated MIG–INP10 pair constitutes a distinct evolutionary lineage.
steps_raw: All 12 of the human CXC chemokine genes were physically mapped using gene-specific PCR primers and the GenBridge 4 radiation hybrid panel. Nine genes, PF4, PF4V1, GRO1, GCP2, PPBP, IL8, GRO2, GRO3, and SCYB5, were assigned within a 1.8-cR interval of one another on 4q. Two additional genes, MIG and INP10, map within 0.5 cR of each another and 6 cR distal to the above-mentioned group. The final gene, SDF1, is localized on 10q. Phylogenetic analyses of amino acid sequences revealed that SDF1 is the most divergent member and that the physically separated MIG–INP10 pair constitutes a distinct evolutionary lineage.
